channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel and Hamas are set to swap more hostages for prisoners in another test of the Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel and Hamas were expected to swap more hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, the second such exchange since a ceasefire began in the Gaza Strip last weekend and another test for the deal.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Indiana attorney general files lawsuit against sheriff for defying feds on illegal immigration
item.description: Indiana Attorney General Todd Rokita's office is suing the St. Joseph County Sheriff's Department in South Bend, Indiana, for refusing to comply with ICE over immigration orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump visit is 'morale boost': CA fire official
item.description: "I don't know what Trump can give us, but it's a morale boost," said Cal Fire battalion chief Brent Pascua.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico busing deported Venezuelans away from border
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – U.S. authorities on Friday removed dozens of migrants through a border crossing in Santa Teresa, New Mexico, and Mexican authorities immediately placed them on buses and drove them south, away from Juarez.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arizona woman charged with murder of rideshare driver after allegedly using his car to run him over: report
item.description: An Arizona woman is accused of running over a 74-year-old rideshare driver multiple times with his car and was charged with second-degree muder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: A man in the central Oregon city of Bend says he was responsible for some of the googly eyes placed on sculptures there.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A 21-year-old Washington state woman faces two weapons charges in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rhode Island judge specializing in immigration law resigns after FBI raids office
item.description: The FBI and Providence, Rhode Island, police raided a Central Falls Municipal Court judge's office Thursday, though the reasons are unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Snowmen replace sandcastles on Alabama beach after historic winter storm
item.description: Many people who don't normally get to experience snow found unique ways to enjoy the rare sight this week as a historic winter storm moved across the South.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas man says he ‘accidentally’ shot girlfriend in head before driving with body for almost 2 hours: police
item.description: A man who drove around with his dead girlfriend in his passenger seat for hours asked his friend, who called 911 to report him, to help him get rid of her body in the desert after he had "accidentally" shot her in the back of the head, police documents said.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: An estimated 24.6 million watched the inauguration, the smallest audience since 2013
item.description: The Nielsen Company said viewership was down from Joe Biden's 2021 inauguration, which reached 33.8 million, and Trump's first move into the White House, seen by 30.6 million in 2017.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cat mistakenly left in plane cargo hold makes 3 trips in 24 hours
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump targets Alaska's oil and other resources as environmentalists gear up for a fight
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — President Donald Trump's expansive executive order aimed at boosting oil and gas drilling, mining and logging in Alaska is being cheered by state political leaders who see new fossil fuel development as critical to Alaska's economic future and criticized by environmental groups
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans renew efforts to limit people in US illegally from census count
item.description: Republican efforts to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states have begun anew, with four Republican state attorneys general suing to alter the once-a-decade head count even before President Donald Trump’s second term in office began Monday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A frigid storm drops rare snow on Houston and New Orleans as Florida readies plows in the Panhandle
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A rare frigid storm charged through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday, blanketing New Orleans and Houston with snow that closed highways, grounded nearly all flights and canceled school for more than a million students more accustomed to hurricane dismissals than snow d
item.dc:creator: GERALD HERBERT and DAVID J. PHILLIP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested at Utah nursing home decades after Hawaii murder
item.description: A man was arrested at a Utah nursing home on suspicion of second-degree murder in connection to a decades-old case from Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, ABC 4

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Colorado woman ID'ed as driver in crash that killed officer
item.description: The driver of a vehicle that collided head on with a Navasota police vehicle killing Sgt. Mark Butler has been identified as 59-year-old Kimberly Lannigan, of Colorado Springs, Colorado. She was also killed in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont near Canada
item.description: Authorities on Tuesday were investigating a fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent that also left a suspect dead and another injured on a Vermont highway near Canada, authorities said. Agent David Maland was killed Monday afternoon following a traffic stop, a U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elephants can't pursue their release from a Colorado zoo because they're not human, court says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Five elephants at a Colorado zoo may be “majestic” but, since they're not human, they do not have the legal right to pursue their release, Colorado’s highest court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: More than 200 potential jurors summoned for trial of prosecutor in Ahmaud Arbery's death
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — More than 200 potential jurors were summoned Tuesday to a Georgia courthouse for questioning about whether they can serve impartially in the trial of a former prosecutor accused of meddling with police as they investigated the 2020 killing of Ahmaud Arbery.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4
